mission.title: keylime: keystore password is required when adding compute into keylime_verifier with keylime_tenant
mission.description: keystore password is required when adding compute into keylime_verifier with keylime_tenant. Requested by 0+ users.

steps.title: Check current keylime deployment
steps.description:
Verify your keylime version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime
helm list -n keylime 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working keylime installation.

steps.title: Review keylime configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keylime configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime
kubectl get configmap -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keylime
```
Keylime CA component requires a password set in its config (ca.conf)

steps.title: Apply the fix for keystore password is required when adding compute into
steps.description:
This is a minimalistic fix for the issue of keystore certificate
passords. Instead of prompting an user to enter a password during the
exection of commands such as `keylime_ca --command init` or
`keylime_tenant -c add ... --cert <cert dir>`, the new code now simply
reads it from
```yaml
# Setup for a user a Keylime credential (only needed one time)
export SYSTEMD_CREDENTIAL_SECRET=$HOME/.config/systemd/credential.secret
systemd-creds setup
systemd-ask-password -n | systemd-creds encrypt - keylime.cred

# I can wrap this in a shell script, or create an alias
KEYLIME_TENANT_PASSWORD=$(systemd-creds decrypt keylime.cred) keylime_tenant ...
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime
kubectl get events -n keylime --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "keystore password is required when adding compute into" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This is a minimalistic fix for the issue of keystore certificate
passords. Instead of prompting an user to enter a password during the
exection of commands such as `keylime_ca --command init` or
`keylime_tenant -c add ... --cert <cert dir>`, the new code now simply
reads it from `/etc/keylime/ca.conf`.
codeSnippets:
# Setup for a user a Keylime credential (only needed one time)
export SYSTEMD_CREDENTIAL_SECRET=$HOME/.config/systemd/credential.secret
systemd-creds setup
systemd-ask-password -n | systemd-creds encrypt - keylime.cred

# I can wrap this in a shell script, or create an alias
KEYLIME_TENANT_PASSWORD=$(systemd-creds decrypt keylime.cred) keylime_tenant ...

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keylime installed or the issue environment reproducible.
